source_note: Structured case package schema for agent or product export. Use after case-intake, safety-guardrails, layoff-defense, compensation-calculator, local-rules-adapter, evidence-builder, negotiation-coach, agreement-review, and arbitration-drafter have produced their draft outputs.

case_snapshot.purpose: Freeze the current known facts and open questions before advice, negotiation, or filing materials are generated.

fact_timeline.purpose: Turn chats, notices, meetings, payment records, and signing deadlines into a dated chronology that separates facts from assumptions.

termination_assessment.purpose: Record the current legal characterization, competing employer/worker characterizations, and what evidence would change the conclusion.

money_summary.purpose: Give a reproducible table of confirmed, possible, and disputed money items with formulas and source anchors.

evidence_directory.purpose: Export a prioritized evidence table with collection status, lawful source, proof purpose, and claim linkage.

negotiation_plan.purpose: Package the settlement floor, ask range, message blocks, forbidden wording, and decision point for switching to arbitration.

agreement_review_summary.purpose: List risky clauses, revisions needed before signing, payment safeguards, and preserved claims.

arbitration_draft_pack.purpose: Prepare the filing-ready structure without claiming it is a final lawyer-reviewed pleading.

safety_and_review_notes.purpose: Keep redline decisions, lawful-evidence limits, unsupported assumptions, and lawyer/local verification items visible in the exported package.

pre_signing_72h.use_when: Worker has not signed resignation, settlement, waiver, handover confirmation, or termination agreement.

arbitration_ready.use_when: Negotiation failed or limitation/payment/evidence risks require filing preparation.

full_case_package.use_when: Agent or product exports a complete working file for user review, lawyer consultation, negotiation, or arbitration preparation.

quality_gates: Every exported money item must include formula, inputs, status, and source anchors.
Every evidence item must state lawful source and collection limit.
Open questions and local_verify/lawyer_check items must stay visible; do not bury them in notes.
Negotiation or arbitration text must not guarantee outcome or encourage unlawful evidence collection.
The package is a working file and draft generator, not a formal lawyer opinion.

Arbitration application materials must remain blocked from direct filing until local form, jurisdiction, limitation, evidence attachments, respondent identity, and lawyer/local professional review checks are complete.
